LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SONIA G. MARTINEZ MOTION FOR ORDER APPOINTING GUARDIAN AD LITEM FOR DEFENDANT MARGUERITE P. PROLL. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE PRESENTED TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT CANNOT UNDERSTAND THE NATURE OF THE LAWSUIT OR THAT SHE CANNOT COMMUNICATE EFFECTIVELY WITH HER COUNSEL. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Apr-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Craig C. Daniel, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: SONIA MARTINEZ
Michael Ng, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Marguerite Proll

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF SONIA MARTINEZ's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery is granted. Responses and production shall be made within 10 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Ntc And Mtn For Order Appointing Guardian Ad Litem For Deft ; Memo Of P And A; Declaration
Off Calendar Dec-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SONIA G. MARTINEZ Motion for Order Appointing Guardian Ad Litem for Defendant Marguerite P. Proll. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Insufficient evidence presented to show that defendant cannot understand the nature of the lawsuit or that she cannot communicate effectively with her counsel. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Off Calendar Nov-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARGUERITE PROLL'S Motion To Transfer Action (Superior Court of San Mateo County) And For Sanctions.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENY TRANSFER AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/PHA/VC) JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
